response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong distributed-systems and backend expertise from roles at Dunnhumby and RIDE Capital, the resume lacks critical hard requirements for a fintech agentic-AI CTO: no demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems experience beyond recent onboarding, no track record scaling engineering teams to 10-30+ people, and no fintech infrastructure background. Current agentic AI work at re:cinq is insufficiently detailed to validate CTO-level capability in this domain.
strengths: 20+ years of backend and distributed-systems expertise, including real-time microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and performance-critical databases
Event-driven architecture and DevOps infrastructure experience demonstrated through GISCollective platform, GitLab administration, CI/CD setup, and Docker deployment
Currently embedded in AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq with hands-on exposure to MCP frameworks and agentic workflows
concerns: No demonstrated production experience operating multi-agent AI systems at scale; current agentic AI work at re:cinq lacks concrete details on system design, outcomes, or production usage

No evidence of scaling engineering teams to organizational leadership level; career shows individual contributor and small-team roles but no track record building senior technical layers or managing 10+ engineers
Zero fintech infrastructure experience; no background in credit systems, payment flows, working-capital management, or regulated financial environments despite these being core CTO responsibilities

key_factors.factor: Track record scaling engineering teams to 10-30+ people
Distributed systems and real-time backend expertise
